title: A small market near my home
summary: Describe the stalls, people, and things you buy at a small market near your home.
topic: A small market near my home
prompt: Write 6–8 short sentences about a small market near your home. Describe the stalls, the people, and what you usually buy. Use present simple and 'there is / there are'.
hintStarters: There is a small market near…
sampleText: There is a small market near my home in Porto, Portugal. It is open every Saturday morning. There are many stalls with fruit, vegetables, fish, and bread. The stalls have canvas roofs in red and yellow. There is a woman who sells tomatoes and peppers. She always weighs them on a small metal scale. She is friendly and she knows many people in the neighborhood. I usually buy a paper bag of potatoes and some fresh herbs. The herbs smell very strong and good. There is also a bread stall next to the gate. The bread is warm and it comes out of the oven early in the morning. I sometimes buy two loaves because the bread is so fresh. The market is crowded but the people are calm. Neighbors stop and talk. Children walk beside their parents. I always feel happy at the market because it is full of familiar faces and good food. I carry my bag home slowly and I feel ready for the week.

sampleAnnotations.phrase: There is a small market near my home
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Có một khu chợ nhỏ gần nhà tôi

sampleAnnotations.phrase: many stalls with fruit, vegetables, fish, and bread
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: nhiều quầy hàng bán trái cây, rau củ, cá và bánh mì

sampleAnnotations.phrase: canvas roofs in red and yellow
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: mái che bằng vải bạt màu đỏ và vàng

sampleAnnotations.phrase: weighs them on a small metal scale
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: cân chúng trên một chiếc cân kim loại nhỏ
một túi giấy đựng khoai tây

sampleAnnotations.phrase: The herbs smell very strong and good
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: Rau thơm có mùi rất đậm và tốt

sampleAnnotations.phrase: warm and it comes out of the oven
crowded but the people are calm
sampleAnnotations.meaningVi: đông đúc nhưng mọi người bình thản
xách túi về nhà từ từ

sampleGrammarNotes.title: There is / There are
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng 'there is' với danh từ số ít và 'there are' với danh từ số nhiều để mô tả những gì tồn tại ở một nơi: 'There is a woman', 'There are many stalls'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use 'there is' with singular nouns and 'there are' with plural nouns to say what exists in a place: 'There is a woman', 'There are many stalls'.

sampleGrammarNotes.title: Present simple to describe a place
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyVi: Dùng thì hiện tại đơn cho những điều luôn đúng về một địa điểm: 'It is open every Saturday', 'She always weighs them', 'I usually buy'.
sampleGrammarNotes.bodyEn: Use the present simple for things that are always true about a place: 'It is open every Saturday', 'She always weighs them', 'I usually buy'.

sampleTranslationVi: Có một khu chợ nhỏ gần nhà tôi ở Porto, Bồ Đào Nha. Chợ mở cửa mỗi sáng thứ Bảy. Có nhiều quầy hàng bán trái cây, rau củ, cá và bánh mì. Các quầy hàng có mái che bằng vải bạt màu đỏ và vàng. Có một người phụ nữ bán cà chua và ớt. Cô luôn cân chúng trên một chiếc cân kim loại nhỏ. Cô thân thiện và biết nhiều người trong khu phố. Tôi thường mua một túi giấy đựng khoai tây và một ít rau thơm tươi. Rau thơm có mùi rất đậm và tốt. Cũng có một quầy bánh mì gần cổng. Bánh mì còn ấm và vừa ra lò từ sáng sớm. Đôi khi tôi mua hai ổ vì bánh mì rất tươi. Chợ đông đúc nhưng mọi người bình thản. Hàng xóm dừng lại và trò chuyện. Trẻ em đi bên cạnh cha mẹ. Tôi luôn cảm thấy vui ở chợ vì nơi đây đầy những khuôn mặt quen thuộc và đồ ăn ngon. Tôi xách túi về nhà từ từ và cảm thấy sẵn sàng cho cả tuần.

options: There is many stalls at the market.
There is a bread stall near the gate.
There is some tomatoes on the table.
There are a woman who sells fish.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There is' is correct with a singular noun: 'a bread stall'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look at the noun after 'there is' — is it singular or plural?

options: There are a big market in my town.
There are many stalls with fruit and bread.
There are a woman who sells herbs.
There are a gate at the front.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'There are' is correct with a plural noun: 'many stalls'.
mcQuestions.hint: Look for a plural noun after 'there are'.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence best describes something at a market?
options: The bread is warm and it comes out of the oven.
The bread is warm and it comes out of the car.
The bread is cold and it comes out of the fridge.
The bread is old and nobody wants it.
mcQuestions.explanation: Fresh market bread comes warm from the oven — this is the most natural description.
mcQuestions.hint: Where does fresh bread at a market come from?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence uses an adverb of frequency correctly?
options: She know always many people.
She always knows many people.
Always she knows many people.
She knows many always people.
mcQuestions.explanation: The adverb 'always' goes before the main verb: 'she always knows'.
mcQuestions.hint: The adverb sits between the subject and the main verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which word fits: 'I usually buy a ___ bag of potatoes.'
options: plastic
paper
metal
glass
mcQuestions.explanation: Markets commonly use paper bags — it is the most natural collocation here.
mcQuestions.hint: Think about what kind of bag a market vendor would use.

options: The market is crowded and it is noisy.
The market is crowded so I leave early.
The market is crowded but the people are calm.
The market is crowded because many people come.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'but' shows contrast: crowded (expected noisy/stressful) but calm (surprising).
mcQuestions.hint: Look for two ideas that go against each other.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which option correctly uses the present simple third person singular?
options: She weigh the tomatoes every day.
She weighs the tomatoes every day.
She is weigh the tomatoes every day.
She weighing the tomatoes every day.
mcQuestions.explanation: Third person singular 'she' adds -s to the verb: 'she weighs'.
mcQuestions.hint: What ending does a verb take with 'she' in the present simple?

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence gives a reason using 'because'?
options: I buy two loaves because so fresh.
I buy two loaves because fresh bread.
I buy two loaves because the bread is so fresh.
Because bread I buy two loaves fresh.
mcQuestions.explanation: 'because' must be followed by a full clause: 'because the bread is so fresh'.
mcQuestions.hint: After 'because', you need a subject and a verb.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence correctly describes what 'familiar faces' means in context?
options: People you see for the first time
People you do not know at all
People you know and see regularly
People who wear hats and masks
mcQuestions.explanation: 'Familiar faces' means people you recognize because you see them often.

mcQuestions.prompt: Which sentence sounds most natural when describing a neighborhood market?
options: People stop and talk and children walk beside their parents.
People is stopping and talking and children is walking.
People stopped and talked and children walked.
People will stop and talk next time.
mcQuestions.explanation: Present simple describes what always happens at the market — it is a regular scene.
mcQuestions.hint: Which tense is used for things that happen regularly?

criticalThinkingQuestion: Why do small local markets feel different from large supermarkets, and which do you prefer for buying food?
